1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class DeleteAccountService < BaseService
4:   include Payloadable
5: 
6:   ASSOCIATIONS_ON_SUSPEND = %w(
7:     account_notes
8:     account_pins
9:     active_relationships
10:     aliases
11:     block_relationships
12:     blocked_by_relationships
13:     conversation_mutes
14:     conversations
15:     custom_filters
16:     domain_blocks
17:     featured_tags
18:     follow_requests
19:     list_accounts
20:     migrations
21:     mute_relationships
22:     muted_by_relationships
23:     notifications
24:     owned_lists
25:     passive_relationships
26:     report_notes
27:     scheduled_statuses
28:     status_pins
29:   ).freeze
30: 
31:   # The following associations have no important side-effects
32:   # in callbacks and all of their own associations are secured
33:   # by foreign keys, making them safe to delete without loading
34:   # into memory
35:   ASSOCIATIONS_WITHOUT_SIDE_EFFECTS = %w(
36:     account_notes
37:     account_pins
38:     aliases
39:     conversation_mutes
40:     conversations
41:     custom_filters
42:     domain_blocks
43:     featured_tags
44:     follow_requests
45:     list_accounts
46:     migrations
47:     mute_relationships
48:     muted_by_relationships
49:     notifications
50:     owned_lists
51:     scheduled_statuses
52:     status_pins
53:     tag_follows
54:   ).freeze
55: 
56:   ASSOCIATIONS_ON_DESTROY = %w(
57:     reports
58:     targeted_moderation_notes
59:     targeted_reports
60:     severed_relationships
61:     remote_severed_relationships
62:   ).freeze
63: 
64:   # Suspend or remove an account and remove as much of its data
65:   # as possible. If it's a local account and it has not been confirmed
66:   # or never been approved, then side effects are skipped and both
67:   # the user and account records are removed fully. Otherwise,
68:   # it is controlled by options.
69:   # @param [Account]
70:   # @param [Hash] options
71:   # @option [Boolean] :reserve_email Keep user record. Only applicable for local accounts
72:   # @option [Boolean] :reserve_username Keep account record
73:   # @option [Boolean] :skip_side_effects Side effects are ActivityPub and streaming API payloads
74:   # @option [Boolean] :skip_activitypub Skip sending ActivityPub payloads. Implied by :skip_side_effects
75:   # @option [Time]    :suspended_at Only applicable when :reserve_username is true
76:   # @option [RelationshipSeveranceEvent] :relationship_severance_event Event used to record severed relationships not initiated by the user
77:   def call(account, **options)
78:     @account = account
79:     @options = { reserve_username: true, reserve_email: true }.merge(options)
80: 
81:     if @account.local? && @account.user_unconfirmed_or_pending?
82:       @options[:reserve_email]     = false
83:       @options[:reserve_username]  = false
84:       @options[:skip_side_effects] = true
85:     end
86: 
87:     @options[:skip_activitypub] = true if @options[:skip_side_effects]
88: 
89:     record_severed_relationships!
90:     distribute_activities!
91:     purge_content!
92:     fulfill_deletion_request!
93:   end
94: 
95:   private
96: 
97:   def distribute_activities!
98:     return if skip_activitypub?
99: 
100:     if @account.local?
101:       delete_actor!
102:     elsif @account.activitypub?
103:       reject_follows!
104:       undo_follows!
105:     end
106:   end
107: 
108:   def reject_follows!
109:     # When deleting a remote account, the account obviously doesn't
110:     # actually become deleted on its origin server, i.e. unlike a
111:     # locally deleted account it continues to have access to its home
112:     # feed and other content. To prevent it from being able to continue
113:     # to access toots it would receive because it follows local accounts,
114:     # we have to force it to unfollow them.
115: 
116:     ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker.push_bulk(Follow.where(account: @account)) do |follow|
117:       [serialize_payload(follow, ActivityPub::RejectFollowSerializer).to_json, follow.target_account_id, @account.inbox_url]
118:     end
119:   end
120: 
121:   def undo_follows!
122:     # When deleting a remote account, the account obviously doesn't
123:     # actually become deleted on its origin server, but following relationships
124:     # are severed on our end. Therefore, make the remote server aware that the
125:     # follow relationships are severed to avoid confusion and potential issues
126:     # if the remote account gets un-suspended.
127: 
128:     ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker.push_bulk(Follow.where(target_account: @account)) do |follow|
129:       [serialize_payload(follow, ActivityPub::UndoFollowSerializer).to_json, follow.account_id, @account.inbox_url]
130:     end
131:   end
132: 
133:   def purge_user!
134:     return if !@account.local? || @account.user.nil?
135: 
136:     if keep_user_record?
137:       @account.user.disable!
138:       @account.user.invites.where(uses: 0).destroy_all
139:     else
140:       @account.user.destroy
141:     end
142:   end
143: 
144:   def purge_content!
145:     purge_user!
146:     purge_profile!
147:     purge_statuses!
148:     purge_mentions!
149:     purge_media_attachments!
150:     purge_polls!
151:     purge_generated_notifications!
152:     purge_favourites!
153:     purge_bookmarks!
154:     purge_feeds!
155:     purge_other_associations!
156: 
157:     @account.destroy unless keep_account_record?
158:   end
159: 
160:   def purge_statuses!
161:     @account.statuses.reorder(nil).where.not(id: reported_status_ids).in_batches do |statuses|
162:       BatchedRemoveStatusService.new.call(statuses, skip_side_effects: skip_side_effects?)
163:     end
164:   end
165: 
166:   def purge_mentions!
167:     @account.mentions.reorder(nil).where.not(status_id: reported_status_ids).in_batches.delete_all
168:   end
169: 
170:   def purge_media_attachments!
171:     @account.media_attachments.find_each do |media_attachment|
172:       next if keep_account_record? && reported_status_ids.include?(media_attachment.status_id)
173: 
174:       media_attachment.destroy
175:     end
176:   end
177: 
178:   def purge_polls!
179:     @account.polls.reorder(nil).where.not(status_id: reported_status_ids).in_batches.delete_all
180:   end
181: 
182:   def purge_generated_notifications!
183:     # By deleting polls and statuses without callbacks, we've left behind
184:     # polymorphically associated notifications generated by this account
185: 
186:     Notification.where(from_account: @account).in_batches.delete_all
187:     NotificationRequest.where(from_account: @account).in_batches.delete_all
188:   end
189: 
190:   def purge_favourites!
191:     @account.favourites.in_batches do |favourites|
192:       ids = favourites.pluck(:status_id)
193:       StatusStat.where(status_id: ids).update_all('favourites_count = GREATEST(0, favourites_count - 1)')
194:       Chewy.strategy.current.update(StatusesIndex, ids) if Chewy.enabled?
195:       Rails.cache.delete_multi(ids.map { |id| "statuses/#{id}" })
196:       favourites.delete_all
197:     end
198:   end
199: 
200:   def purge_bookmarks!
201:     @account.bookmarks.in_batches do |bookmarks|
202:       Chewy.strategy.current.update(StatusesIndex, bookmarks.pluck(:status_id)) if Chewy.enabled?
203:       bookmarks.delete_all
204:     end
205:   end
206: 
207:   def purge_other_associations!
208:     associations_for_destruction.each do |association_name|
209:       purge_association(association_name)
210:     end
211:   end
212: 
213:   def purge_feeds!
214:     return unless @account.local?
215: 
216:     FeedManager.instance.clean_feeds!(:home, [@account.id])
217:     FeedManager.instance.clean_feeds!(:list, @account.owned_lists.pluck(:id))
218:   end
219: 
220:   def purge_profile!
221:     # If the account is going to be destroyed
222:     # there is no point wasting time updating
223:     # its values first
224: 
225:     return unless keep_account_record?
226: 
227:     @account.silenced_at         = nil
228:     @account.suspended_at        = @options[:suspended_at] || Time.now.utc
229:     @account.suspension_origin   = :local
230:     @account.locked              = false
231:     @account.memorial            = false
232:     @account.discoverable        = false
233:     @account.trendable           = false
234:     @account.display_name        = ''
235:     @account.note                = ''
236:     @account.fields              = []
237:     @account.statuses_count      = 0
238:     @account.followers_count     = 0
239:     @account.following_count     = 0
240:     @account.moved_to_account    = nil
241:     @account.reviewed_at         = nil
242:     @account.requested_review_at = nil
243:     @account.also_known_as       = []
244:     @account.avatar.destroy
245:     @account.header.destroy
246:     @account.save!
247:   end
248: 
249:   def fulfill_deletion_request!
250:     @account.deletion_request&.destroy

(Showing lines 1-250 of 330. Use offset=251 to continue.)

<path>/Users/luc/Developer/luuuc/oss/sense-benchmark/baseline/mastodon/app/services/suspend_account_service.rb</path>
<type>file</type>
<content>
1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class SuspendAccountService < BaseService
4:   include Payloadable
5: 
6:   # Carry out the suspension of a recently-suspended account
7:   # @param [Account] account Account to suspend
8:   def call(account)
9:     return unless account.suspended?
10: 
11:     @account = account
12: 
13:     reject_remote_follows!
14:     distribute_update_actor!
15:     unmerge_from_home_timelines!
16:     unmerge_from_list_timelines!
17:     privatize_media_attachments!
18:     remove_from_trends!
19:   end
20: 
21:   private
22: 
23:   def reject_remote_follows!
24:     return if @account.local? || !@account.activitypub? || @account.suspension_origin_remote?
25: 
26:     # When suspending a remote account, the account obviously doesn't
27:     # actually become suspended on its origin server, i.e. unlike a
28:     # locally suspended account it continues to have access to its home
29:     # feed and other content. To prevent it from being able to continue
30:     # to access toots it would receive because it follows local accounts,
31:     # we have to force it to unfollow them. Unfortunately, there is no
32:     # counterpart to this operation, i.e. you can't then force a remote
33:     # account to re-follow you, so this part is not reversible.
34: 
35:     Follow.where(account: @account).find_in_batches do |follows|
36:       ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker.push_bulk(follows) do |follow|
37:         [serialize_payload(follow, ActivityPub::RejectFollowSerializer).to_json, follow.target_account_id, @account.inbox_url]
38:       end
39: 
40:       follows.each(&:destroy)
41:     end
42:   end
43: 
44:   def distribute_update_actor!
45:     return unless @account.local?
46: 
47:     account_reach_finder = AccountReachFinder.new(@account)
48: 
49:     ActivityPub::DeliveryWorker.push_bulk(account_reach_finder.inboxes, limit: 1_000) do |inbox_url|
50:       [signed_activity_json, @account.id, inbox_url]
51:     end
52:   end
53: 
54:   def unmerge_from_home_timelines!
55:     @account.followers_for_local_distribution.reorder(nil).find_each do |follower|
56:       FeedManager.instance.unmerge_from_home(@account, follower)
57:     end
58:   end
59: 
60:   def unmerge_from_list_timelines!
61:     @account.lists_for_local_distribution.reorder(nil).find_each do |list|
62:       FeedManager.instance.unmerge_from_list(@account, list)
63:     end
64:   end
65: 
66:   def privatize_media_attachments!
67:     UpdateMediaAttachmentsPermissionsService.new.call(@account.media_attachments, :private)
68:   end
69: 
70:   def remove_from_trends!
71:     StatusTrend.where(account: @account).delete_all
72:   end
73: 
74:   def signed_activity_json
75:     @signed_activity_json ||= serialize_payload(@account, ActivityPub::UpdateActorSerializer, signer: @account).to_json
76:   end
77: end

(End of file - total 77 lines)
</content>

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class AccountStatusesCleanupService < BaseService
4:   # @param [AccountStatusesCleanupPolicy] account_policy
5:   # @param [Integer] budget
6:   # @return [Integer]
7:   def call(account_policy, budget = 50)
8:     return 0 unless account_policy.enabled?
9: 
10:     cutoff_id = account_policy.compute_cutoff_id
11:     return 0 if cutoff_id.blank?
12: 
13:     num_deleted = 0
14:     last_deleted = nil
15: 
16:     account_policy.statuses_to_delete(budget, cutoff_id, account_policy.last_inspected).reorder(nil).find_each(order: :asc) do |status|
17:       status.discard_with_reblogs
18:       RemovalWorker.perform_async(status.id, { 'redraft' => false })
19:       num_deleted += 1
20:       last_deleted = status.id
21:     end
22: 
23:     account_policy.record_last_inspected(last_deleted.presence || cutoff_id)
24: 
25:     num_deleted
26:   end
27: end

(End of file - total 27 lines)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class Vacuum::StatusesVacuum
4:   include Redisable
5: 
6:   def initialize(retention_period)
7:     @retention_period = retention_period
8:   end
9: 
10:   def perform
11:     vacuum_statuses! if @retention_period.present?
12:   end
13: 
14:   private
15: 
16:   def vacuum_statuses!
17:     statuses_scope.in_batches do |statuses|
18:       # Side-effects not covered by foreign keys, such
19:       # as the search index, must be handled first.
20:       statuses.direct_visibility
21:         .includes(mentions: :account)
22:         .find_each(&:unlink_from_conversations!)
23:       if Chewy.enabled?
24:         remove_from_index(statuses.ids, 'chewy:queue:StatusesIndex')
25:         remove_from_index(statuses.ids, 'chewy:queue:PublicStatusesIndex')
26:       end
27: 
28:       # Foreign keys take care of most associated records for us.
29:       # Media attachments will be orphaned.
30:       statuses.delete_all
31:     end
32:   end
33: 
34:   def statuses_scope
35:     Status.unscoped.kept
36:       .joins(:account).merge(Account.remote)
37:       .where(statuses: { id: ...retention_period_as_id })
38:   end
39: 
40:   def retention_period_as_id
41:     Mastodon::Snowflake.id_at(@retention_period.ago, with_random: false)
42:   end
43: 
44:   def remove_from_index(status_ids, index)
45:     with_redis { |redis| redis.sadd(index, status_ids) }
46:   end
47: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class ActivityPub::Activity::Create < ActivityPub::Activity
4:   DISTRIBUTE_DELAY = 1.minute
5:   PROCESSING_DELAY = (30.seconds)..(10.minutes)
6: 
7:   def perform
8:     @account.schedule_refresh_if_stale!
9: 
10:     dereference_object!
11: 
12:     create_status
13:   end
14: 
15:   private
16: 
17:   def create_status
18:     return reject_payload! if unsupported_object_type? || non_matching_uri_hosts?(@account.uri, object_uri) || tombstone_exists? || !related_to_local_activity?
19: 
20:     with_redis_lock("create:#{object_uri}") do
21:       Status.uncached do
22:         return if delete_arrived_first?(object_uri) || poll_vote?
23: 
24:         @status = find_existing_status
25:       end
26: 
27:       if @status.nil?
28:         process_status
29:       elsif @options[:delivered_to_account_id].present?
30:         postprocess_audience_and_deliver
31:       end
32:     end
33: 
34:     @status
35:   end
36: 
37:   def audience_to
38:     as_array(@object['to'] || @json['to']).map { |x| value_or_id(x) }
39:   end
40: 
41:   def audience_cc
42:     as_array(@object['cc'] || @json['cc']).map { |x| value_or_id(x) }
43:   end
44: 
45:   def process_status
46:     @tags                 = []
47:     @mentions             = []
48:     @tagged_objects       = []
49:     @unresolved_mentions  = []
50:     @unresolved_collections = []
51:     @silenced_account_ids = []
52:     @params               = {}
53:     @quote                = nil
54:     @quote_uri            = nil
55:     @quote_approval_uri   = nil
56: 
57:     process_status_params
58:     process_tags
59:     process_quote
60:     process_audience
61: 
62:     ApplicationRecord.transaction do
63:       @status = Status.create!(@params.merge(quote: @quote))
64:       attach_tags(@status)
65:       attach_tagged_objects(@status)
66:       attach_mentions(@status)
67:       attach_counts(@status)
68:     end
69: 
70:     resolve_thread(@status)
71:     resolve_unresolved_mentions(@status)
72:     resolve_unresolved_collections(@status)
73:     fetch_replies(@status)
74:     fetch_and_verify_quote
75:     distribute
76:     forward_for_reply
77:   end
78: 
79:   def distribute
80:     # Spread out crawling randomly to avoid DDoSing the link
81:     LinkCrawlWorker.perform_in(rand(DISTRIBUTE_DELAY), @status.id)
82: 
83:     # Distribute into home and list feeds and notify mentioned accounts
84:     ::DistributionWorker.perform_async(@status.id, { 'silenced_account_ids' => @silenced_account_ids }) if @options[:override_timestamps] || @status.within_realtime_window?
85:   end
86: 
87:   def find_existing_status
88:     status   = status_from_uri(object_uri)
89:     status ||= Status.find_by(uri: @object['atomUri']) if @object['atomUri'].present?
90:     status if status&.account_id == @account.id
91:   end
92: 
93:   def process_status_params
94:     @status_parser = ActivityPub::Parser::StatusParser.new(
95:       @json,
96:       followers_collection: @account.followers_url,
97:       following_collection: @account.following_url,
98:       actor_uri: ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.uri_for(@account),
99:       object: @object
100:     )
101: 
102:     attachment_ids = process_attachments.take(Status::MEDIA_ATTACHMENTS_LIMIT).map(&:id)
103: 
104:     @params = {
105:       uri: @status_parser.uri,
106:       url: @status_parser.url || @status_parser.uri,
107:       account: @account,
108:       text: @status_parser.processed_text,
109:       language: @status_parser.language,
110:       spoiler_text: @status_parser.processed_spoiler_text,
111:       created_at: @status_parser.created_at,
112:       edited_at: @status_parser.edited_at && @status_parser.edited_at != @status_parser.created_at ? @status_parser.edited_at : nil,
113:       override_timestamps: @options[:override_timestamps],
114:       reply: @status_parser.reply,
115:       sensitive: @account.sensitized? || @status_parser.sensitive || false,
116:       visibility: @status_parser.visibility,
117:       thread: replied_to_status,
118:       conversation: conversation_from_uri(@object['conversation']),
119:       media_attachment_ids: attachment_ids,
120:       ordered_media_attachment_ids: attachment_ids,
121:       poll: process_poll,
122:       quote_approval_policy: @status_parser.quote_policy,
123:     }
124:   end
125: 
126:   def process_audience
127:     # Unlike with tags, there is no point in resolving accounts we don't already
128:     # know here, because silent mentions would only be used for local access control anyway
129:     accounts_in_audience = (audience_to + audience_cc).uniq.filter_map do |audience|
130:       account_from_uri(audience) unless ActivityPub::TagManager.instance.public_collection?(audience)
131:     end
132: 
133:     # If the payload was delivered to a specific inbox, the inbox owner must have
134:     # access to it, unless they already have access to it anyway
135:     if @options[:delivered_to_account_id]
136:       accounts_in_audience << delivered_to_account
137:       accounts_in_audience.uniq!
138:     end
139: 
140:     accounts_in_audience.each do |account|
141:       # This runs after tags are processed, and those translate into non-silent
142:       # mentions, which take precedence
143:       next if @mentions.any? { |mention| mention.account_id == account.id }
144: 
145:       @mentions << Mention.new(account: account, silent: true)
146: 
147:       # If there is at least one silent mention, then the status can be considered
148:       # as a limited-audience status, and not strictly a direct message, but only
149:       # if we considered a direct message in the first place
150:       @params[:visibility] = :limited if @params[:visibility] == :direct

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class StatusReachFinder
4:   # @param [Status] status
5:   # @param [Hash] options
6:   # @option options [Boolean] :unsafe
7:   def initialize(status, options = {})
8:     @status  = status
9:     @options = options
10:   end
11: 
12:   def inboxes
13:     (reached_account_inboxes + followers_inboxes + relay_inboxes).uniq
14:   end
15: 
16:   private
17: 
18:   def reached_account_inboxes
19:     scope = Account.where(id: reached_account_ids)
20:     inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
21:   end
22: 
23:   def reached_account_ids
24:     # When the status is a reblog, there are no interactions with it
25:     # directly, we assume all interactions are with the original one
26: 
27:     if @status.reblog?
28:       [reblog_of_account_id]
29:     else
30:       [
31:         replied_to_account_id,
32:         reblog_of_account_id,
33:         quote_of_account_id,
34:         mentioned_account_ids,
35:         reblogs_account_ids,
36:         quotes_account_ids,
37:         favourites_account_ids,
38:         replies_account_ids,
39:       ].tap do |arr|
40:         arr.flatten!
41:         arr.compact!
42:         arr.uniq!
43:       end
44:     end
45:   end
46: 
47:   def replied_to_account_id
48:     @status.in_reply_to_account_id if distributable?
49:   end
50: 
51:   def quote_of_account_id
52:     @status.quote&.quoted_account_id
53:   end
54: 
55:   def reblog_of_account_id
56:     @status.reblog.account_id if @status.reblog?
57:   end
58: 
59:   def mentioned_account_ids
60:     @status.mentions.pluck(:account_id)
61:   end
62: 
63:   # Beware: Quotes can be created without the author having had access to the status
64:   def quotes_account_ids
65:     @status.quotes.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
66:   end
67: 
68:   # Beware: Reblogs can be created without the author having had access to the status
69:   def reblogs_account_ids
70:     @status.reblogs.rewhere(deleted_at: [nil, @status.deleted_at]).pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
71:   end
72: 
73:   # Beware: Favourites can be created without the author having had access to the status
74:   def favourites_account_ids
75:     @status.favourites.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
76:   end
77: 
78:   # Beware: Replies can be created without the author having had access to the status
79:   def replies_account_ids
80:     @status.replies.pluck(:account_id) if distributable? || unsafe?
81:   end
82: 
83:   def followers_inboxes
84:     scope = followers_scope
85:     inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
86:   end
87: 
88:   def relay_inboxes
89:     if @status.public_visibility?
90:       Relay.enabled.pluck(:inbox_url)
91:     else
92:       []
93:     end
94:   end
95: 
96:   def distributable?
97:     @status.public_visibility? || @status.unlisted_visibility?
98:   end
99: 
100:   def unsafe?
101:     @options[:unsafe]
102:   end
103: 
104:   def followers_scope
105:     if @status.in_reply_to_local_account? && distributable?
106:       @status.account.followers.or(@status.thread.account.followers.not_domain_blocked_by_account(@status.account))
107:     elsif @status.direct_visibility? || @status.limited_visibility?
108:       Account.none
109:     else
110:       @status.account.followers
111:     end
112:   end
113: 
114:   def inboxes_without_suspended_for(scope)
115:     scope.merge!(Account.without_suspended) unless unsafe?
116:     scope.inboxes
117:   end
118: end

(End of file - total 118 lines)

    "contract_notes_for_teammates": [
      "Status uses soft-delete via Discard::Model; default_scope hides discarded rows, so callers must use Status.unscoped or Status.with_discarded to see deleted statuses.",
      "The visibility enum and distributable? predicate control every fan-out, federation, search, and streaming decision.",
      "SafeReblogInsert overrides ActiveRecord _insert_record to enforce deleted_at IS NULL at the DB level for reblogs.",
      "Counter caches (status_stat), mentions, media, tags, quotes, and conversation links are all attached data that must be considered on teardown."
    ]

      { "rank": 2, "area": "federation", "file:line": "app/lib/activitypub/activity/create.rb", "risk": "Remote create/update/delete/undo rely on URI-to-Status lookup and visibility predicates; teardown changes can cause duplicate statuses, missing deletes, or leaked private posts." },
      { "rank": 3, "area": "deletion", "file:line": "app/services/remove_status_service.rb", "risk": "Changing how attached data (mentions, media, quotes, reblogs, conversations) is cleaned up will leave orphaned records or broken streaming payloads." },
      { "rank": 4, "area": "search", "file:line": "app/chewy/statuses_index.rb:55", "risk": "StatusesIndex/PublicStatusesIndex rely on kept/without_reblogs/indexable scopes and searchable_by; changes break Elasticsearch consistency." },
      { "rank": 5, "area": "account lifecycle", "file:line": "app/services/delete_account_service.rb:161", "risk": "Batch removal and counter-cache decrements assume status_id relationships; teardown changes may leave stale stats or index entries." },
